ingress_contract_exists.evidence: Ingress is the data migration from research database (ep-young-block-aii5nj6b) via CSV export/import. Migration DDL is declared in migrations/006_ctb_doctrine_tables.sql. Data source documented in db_changeset.json data_migration section. Schema diff declares baseline (absent) → target (present) state.

egress_contract_exists.evidence: Egress is docs/schema/CTB_DOCTRINE_TABLES.md (read-only documentation) and semantic search queries against doctrine.doctrine_library (consumed by SID worker via embedding similarity). Query contract documented in CTB_DOCTRINE_TABLES.md §4 Semantic Search Example and worker_briefing.md Doctrine Library Access section.

no_orphan_tables.evidence: All 4 tables are registered in ctb.table_registry. ctb.table_registry is self-registering (row 4 registers itself). doctrine.doctrine_library is consumed by SID worker for template resolution. doctrine.doctrine_key is consumed by full-text section lookup. doctrine.doctrine_library_error is consumed by error triage. No orphan tables.

no_unconsumed_events.evidence: These tables do not emit events. doctrine.doctrine_library has a trg_set_updated_at trigger but it is an infrastructure trigger (auto-sets updated_at), not a business event. No pg_notify channels. No event emission from ctb or doctrine schemas.

id_propagation_intact.evidence: doctrine_id propagates from doctrine.doctrine_library to doctrine.doctrine_key via first_doctrine_id field (by value). doctrine.doctrine_key sections map back to doctrine_library chunks via domain + major_section + minor_section. ctb.table_registry tracks tables via (table_schema, table_name) composite — propagated by value to any schema enforcement queries. No broken ID chains.
